0.detail: Stock reservation, checkout session, Polar order, settlement, and payout events share one correlation id.
0.title: Unified event chain

1.detail: High-value refunds and manual stock corrections require review before they settle into the merchant statement.
1.title: Exception workflow

2.detail: Operational dashboards track webhook lag, missing balance transactions, oversell risk, and stale bundle inventory.
2.title: Control coverage

audits.description: Audit inventory and payment operations together so support, finance, and operators can explain every stock and money movement.

insights.0: Every payment row should reference the inventory reservation that created it.
insights.1: Every fee row should record source, currency, percentage, fixed amount, and settlement status.
insights.2: Every stock correction should include actor, reason, and before/after quantity.

0.detail: Event chains with inventory, checkout, fee, and payout references.
0.label: Trace coverage

1.detail: Polar webhook to inventory ledger confirmation window.
1.label: Median lag

2.detail: Open exceptions across refunds, stock changes, and settlement drift.
2.label: Exceptions

audits.title: Payment and inventory observability

0.detail: A creator launch kit combines apparel, digital access, and a limited signed item while sharing one checkout promise.
0.title: Creator launch kit

1.detail: Bundle availability follows the scarcest component and prevents selling a promotion that cannot be fulfilled.
1.title: Component stock guard

2.detail: Promotions keep stock, margin, and Polar checkout readiness visible before the campaign is published.
2.title: Promotion economics

bundles.description: Create product bundles and promotions that respect stock constraints, ownership splits, and checkout economics.

insights.0: Reserve component stock atomically when a bundle checkout starts.
insights.1: Use Polar settlement records for Polar platform fees, Stripe fees, and conversion fees.
insights.2: Expire campaign inventory separately from evergreen catalog stock.

1.detail: Revenue lift from bundle checkout sessions.
1.label: Bundle uplift

2.detail: Bundles paused by missing components before oversell.
2.label: Paused

bundles.title: Bundles and promotion operations

0.detail: Each checkout exposes subtotal, reservation status, Polar checkout state, and settlement data once Polar returns it.
0.title: Clear customer and seller fee view

1.detail: Reservations are created before checkout and released automatically when sessions expire or payments fail.
1.title: Inventory-safe checkout

2.detail: Polar checkout and order state should reconcile against inventory reservations so operators understand every settlement difference.
2.title: Fee reconciliation

checkout.description: Design checkout around inventory reservations, Polar checkout state, and settlement reconciliation.

insights.0: Do not invent fees outside Polar and Stripe settlement records that Polar accounts for.
insights.1: Persist Polar checkout and order state instead of local fee estimates.
insights.2: Release inventory when checkout expires, payment fails, or fraud review blocks capture.

0.detail: Checkout sessions with real Polar state.
0.label: State clarity

2.detail: Provider settlement rows reconciled to checkout reservations.
2.label: Matched settlements

checkout.title: Transparent checkout and settlement

0.detail: Physical products, services, experiences, digital goods, and talent-owned merchandise can live in one catalog with typed attributes.
0.title: Product type taxonomy

1.detail: Items carry owner, talent, supplier, channel, and workspace ownership fields so payouts and inventory authority stay clear.
1.title: Owner and talent mapping

2.detail: Variants track SKU, barcode, fulfillment policy, tax category, and stock policy without splitting the business context.
2.title: SKU and variant layer

items.description: Manage all products in one catalog while keeping type, owner, talent, supplier, and fulfillment metadata visible.

insights.0: Use category, owner, and talent tags independently so one item can power multiple storefront contexts.
insights.1: Keep stock policy on the variant, not only the parent product.
insights.2: Treat digital access and physical goods as different fulfillment policies under the same inventory contract.

0.detail: Products ready for checkout, bundling, or reservation.
0.label: Active items

1.detail: Items grouped by type, owner, talent, supplier, and channel.
1.label: Classified

2.detail: Variants with complete SKU and stock policy metadata.
2.label: Variant health

items.title: Product catalog and ownership

0.detail: Operators can see stock health, checkout reservations, Polar readiness, and settlement reconciliation from the same workspace.
0.title: One operating surface

1.detail: Inventory items can be categorized by product type, owner, talent, supplier, fulfillment policy, and sales channel.
1.title: Flexible categorization

2.detail: Payment, refund, stock, and payout events are designed to be audited together instead of stitched manually later.
2.title: Ledger-first observability

overview.description: A workspace for managing products, stock, bundles, Polar checkout readiness, and operating controls.

insights.0: Inventory reservations should be linked to checkout sessions before payment capture.
insights.1: Polar checkout state belongs in checkout, seller reports, and finance reconciliation.
insights.2: Polar readiness should be treated as a business readiness gate.

0.detail: Sellable units across products, variants, and bundles.
0.label: Available stock

1.detail: Orders protected by live reservations before payment confirmation.
1.label: Reserved orders

2.detail: Storefronts ready to route checkout through Polar.
2.label: Polar-ready stores

overview.title: Inventory and checkout operations

0.detail: Receiving, transfer, reservation, release, adjustment, return, and spoilage events write to the same stock movement timeline.
0.title: Movement ledger

1.detail: Low-stock thresholds can vary by owner, warehouse, channel, talent campaign, and bundle commitment.
1.title: Reorder signals

2.detail: Stock reservations lock sellable quantity while Polar checkout is pending and release cleanly when sessions expire.
2.title: Reservation control

stock.description: Track inventory quantities as a ledger of stock movements rather than a single editable number.

insights.0: Separate available, reserved, damaged, returned, and committed quantities.
insights.1: Use movement reasons instead of silent quantity edits.
insights.2: Expose oversell risk before campaigns and promotions go live.

0.detail: Warehouses, pop-ups, and talent campaign pools.
0.label: Stock locations

1.detail: Items below reorder threshold or committed to pending bundles.
1.label: At risk

2.detail: Movement rows reconciled against checkout reservations.
2.label: Ledger health

stock.title: Stock ledger and reservation control

0.detail: Clients configure workspace-owned Polar checkout tokens so storefront orders can create Polar checkouts.
0.title: Workspace-owned Polar checkout

1.detail: Token validation, checkout product readiness, and environment defaults should block risky checkout flows.
1.title: Business readiness gate

2.detail: Polar order state, refunds, disputes, and payouts need separate ledger rows for seller reporting once settlement data is available.
2.title: Settlement ledger

stripe.connectDescription: The app is registered for inventory.tuturuuu.com and ready to receive centralized Polar checkout routes from apps/web. Protected payment APIs should stay behind apps/web so bot protection, audit logging, and provider secrets remain centralized.
stripe.description: Prepare storefront checkout with workspace-owned Polar tokens, clear reservation state, and payout observability from provider settlement data.

insights.0: Store Polar token fingerprints centrally; never expose provider secrets to the satellite app.
insights.1: Use protected web API boundaries for checkout creation and token validation.
insights.2: Treat Polar order and settlement data as the source of truth for fees after settlement.

0.detail: Workspaces with saved Polar token fingerprints.
0.label: Configured workspaces

1.detail: Environments with a checkout product ready.
1.label: Checkout-ready

2.detail: Orders missing final provider settlement reconciliation.
2.label: Settlement drift

stripe.title: Polar checkout readiness
